title: Fondant Canadian Ricotta Poutine
description: A pescatarian-friendly twist on the classic Canadian dish, featuring creamy ricotta cheese and a drizzle of fondant.
instructions: Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil and cook the crushed potatoes until tender, about 10-12 minutes, then drain and set aside.
In a separate pan, heat the butter over medium heat until melted, then add the grated cheddar cheese and stir until melted.
In a separate pan, heat the fryer oil to 375°F (190°C) for frying the potatoes.
Once the fryer oil is hot, carefully add the cooked potatoes and fry until golden brown, about 3-5 minutes, then drain on paper towels.
In a separate pan, heat the ricotta cheese over low heat until warmed through.
In a separate pan, heat the fondant over low heat until melted and smooth.

To assemble the poutine, place a scoop of the fried potatoes onto a plate, top with a spoonful of the warmed ricotta cheese, and drizzle with the melted fondant, garnished with a sprinkle of bonito flakes and chopped parsley.
Season with salt and pepper to taste, then serve immediately.
Garnish with extra parsley if desired.
